content.claim: Autism spectrum disorder registrations among Hungarian children nearly tripled to quadrupled between 2013 and 2023/24, with conflicting source figures reporting either 3,319→9,737 or 3,061→11,685 depending on reference population
content.context: Fresh live research through the Education finance lens
limitations: Two KSH-sourced figures disagree on both base values and endpoints, indicating either calendar-year versus school-year definitional differences or data-vintage inconsistency
Exact multiplier (threefold vs. fourfold) and interpretation remain unresolved
No single authoritative KSH release URL identified in search
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
